verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family expressed predominantly in testis/sperm. Unlike IZUMO1 which is a single-pass TM protein, IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is a secreted/soluble protein. However, it forms a stable complex with other IZUMO family members (notably IZUMO1) on the sperm surface via disulfide-linked interactions, and is detected on the sperm plasma membrane as part of this complex. Its surface display is restricted to the germline (sperm) lineage. This fits tissue_restricted_surface — narrow germline lineage display via stable complex partnership with a TM anchor (IZUMO1).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether IZUMO4's association with IZUMO1/sperm surface is wash-resistant stable complex vs. transient; gene-specific surface biotinylation evidence is limited.
